title: Publications
text:
Papers and reports produced by the NSW Child E Cohort Program. [Placeholder entries — replace references.bib with the Program’s real publications.]
To add a new publication, add a BibTeX entry to references.bib — no other changes are needed; this page and its reference list update automatically.


Falster, Kathleen, and Author Placeholder. 2024. “Placeholder Title: Early-Life Determinants of Child Health Outcomes in NSW.” Placeholder Journal of Population Health 1 (1): 1–10. https://doi.org/10.0000/placeholder.2024.001.

title: Project Context: NSW Child E Cohort Website
text:
This repo builds the public website for the NSW Child E Cohort Program, based in the UNSW School of Population Health and led by Dr Kathleen Falster.
The design/UX reference point is spcanelon/silvia — a personal academic site built with Quarto, inspired by the Hugo Apéro theme. Navbar sections must be, in order: Projects, People, Mission, Publications, Media.











Decision
Choice
Why




Framework
Quarto
Matches the reference site; markdown/YAML-based so lab members can add people/publications/projects without touching code.


Hosting
GitHub Pages
Free, deploys straight from this repo via GitHub Actions.


Initial content
Placeholders
Structure and styling built now; team bios, real publications, project descriptions, and media items to be swapped in later.


Branding
UNSW branding
UNSW Yellow/Black core palette adapted into the reference site’s clean academic layout, rather than reusing the reference site’s own colors.






_quarto.yml              site config: navbar order, theme, output
index.qmd                Home: hero + highlight cards linking to the 5 sections
mission.qmd              Program mission/aims/background
projects.qmd             Research project listing (placeholder cards)
people.qmd               Team grid (lead, investigators, staff/students)
publications.qmd         Publication list, rendered from references.bib
media.qmd                News/media mentions
styles.scss              Custom SCSS theme (UNSW colors)
assets/images/           logo, favicon, hero image, photo placeholders
.github/workflows/publish.yml   GitHub Actions: render + deploy to gh-pages
references.bib           placeholder BibTeX entries for Publications page




Verify UNSW brand hex codes against the official UNSW brand toolkit before public launch — this build uses commonly published UNSW Yellow (#FFD100) / Black as a reasonable placeholder, not a fetched brand PDF.
Point GitHub Pages at the gh-pages branch in the repo’s Settings → Pages (one-time manual step after the first Actions run creates that branch).
Install the Quarto CLI locally (https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/) to run quarto preview/quarto render on this machine — it wasn’t detected during this build. CI renders independently of this.
Replace placeholder content: team bios/photos, real project descriptions, actual publications (references.bib), and media/news items.

title: Mission
text:
[Placeholder — replace with the Program’s actual mission statement.]
The NSW Child E Cohort Program brings together linked, population-level administrative and health data to understand the early-life factors that shape child health, development, and educational outcomes across New South Wales. The Program is based in the UNSW School of Population Health and led by Dr Kathleen Falster.

title: Projects
text:
Research projects underway within the NSW Child E Cohort Program. [Placeholder content — replace each card with real project details.]


Project One: Early Childhood Development Outcomes
[Placeholder description.] This project examines how early-life factors relate to developmental outcomes measured through the Australian Early Development Census (AEDC) and linked health records.
Status: Ongoing


Project Two: Maternal & Perinatal Health Linkage
[Placeholder description.] Linking perinatal data collections to explore associations between maternal health during pregnancy and child outcomes.
Status: Ongoing


Project Three: Education & Health Data Linkage
[Placeholder description.] Investigating the relationship between early health experiences and later educational attainment using linked NSW datasets.
Status: Planned


Project Four: [Placeholder Title]
[Placeholder description.] Add a short summary of the project’s aims, data sources, and expected outputs here.
Status: [Placeholder]

title: NSW Child E Cohort Program
text:
NSW Child E Cohort Program
Understanding the early life origins of child health and development in New South Wales, through linked, population-level data.



Projects
Explore the research projects underway within the NSW Child E Cohort Program.


People
Meet the investigators, staff, and students leading and supporting this work.
